_README: Copy this whole folder to levels/level-<yourname>/ then edit the values below. The _README and _doc_* keys are notes for you — the engine ignores any key it doesn't recognise, so they won't break anything, but a tidy level deletes them once you understand the shape.
_doc_startPosition: Where the player spawns, in canvas pixels (top-left of the player). Gravity then drops them onto the nearest platform below.
_doc_bounds: The world rectangle. Increase w for a longer level — the camera scrolls within it. Keep h at 540 (the canvas height).
_doc_platforms: Solid ground and ledges. Each is x,y (top-left) + width + height, in pixels. The player stands on, and is blocked by, every platform. Leave gaps between platforms to jump across.
_doc_placements: Where hazards / collectibles / power-ups sit. objectId MUST match an id in data/objects.json (gas-boiler, ice-car, oil-slick, ccgt, peaker, standby-gremlin = hazards; solar-panel, wind-turbine, battery-cell = collectibles; heat-pump, insulation = power-ups). x,y is the top-left in pixels — to rest an object on a platform, set y = (platform top y) - 40.
_doc_actors: Optional moving/active things. DELETE the ones you don't use. Each is placed by data alone — no code. Types: 'mover' = moving platform (axis x|y, distance px, speed px/s) the player rides; 'block' = breakable box, bash from below to shatter, optional drop=<objectId> pops out; 'enemy' = patroller (range px, speed, points), stomp from above to defeat, side-touch kills; 'emitter' = fires lethal bolts (dir -1 left/+1 right, interval s, speed; optional range px = only fire when player near); 'spring' = bounce pad (power = launch speed); 'crumble' = platform that breaks shortly after you land. All x,y are top-left pixels.
_doc_goal: The finish-flag position (top-left, pixels). Put it at the far end of your level.
